title: IndicLens: Search Redefined for Bharat
subtitle: A Zero-to-One Multimodal Discovery Engine for the Next Billion Users
problem_statement: Despite high internet penetration in India, search engines remain a bottleneck for the 'Next Billion Users.' Existing models are English-centric, ad-cluttered, and rely on high literacy levels. Indian users frequently encounter 'Information Asymmetry' where high-quality local insights are buried under SEO-optimized global content. There is a deep psychological friction where users feel alienated by text-heavy interfaces, leading to a reliance on fragmented WhatsApp forwards rather than credible sources.

pillars.title: The Linguistic Barrier
pillars.insight: Users feel a sense of 'Digital Inadequacy' when forced to spell in English, leading to high abandonment rates.

pillars.title: Video-First Preference
pillars.insight: Indian users instinctively trust video demonstrations over written instructions due to higher perceived authenticity.

pillars.title: Verification Anxiety
pillars.insight: Users struggle to distinguish between sponsored content and genuine advice, causing a trust deficit in search results.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we make information accessible to users who prefer voice and visuals over text?
How might we eliminate the 'Ad-Fatigue' and provide verifiable local results?
How might we bridge the linguistic gap for non-native English speakers in Tier 2/3 cities?

personas.name: Ramesh Kumar
personas.quote: I want to grow my business but I don't know the right English words to search for tools.
personas.bio: Ramesh runs a local handicraft shop. He is comfortable with WhatsApp and YouTube but finds traditional search results overwhelming and irrelevant to his local context.

points.action: Mix voice search with real-time camera translation
points.benefit: Users can point at a physical object and ask questions about it instantly.

points.action: Change the list of blue links into interactive visual story cards
points.benefit: Easier to scan and understand without reading heavy text.

points.action: Replace standard ads with verified local service provider badges
points.benefit: Builds trust and helps users find help nearby immediately.
Reduces spelling errors and makes the user feel more confident.

pivot.before: A text-heavy, ad-driven list of global websites that requires high English proficiency.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shifting from 'Indexing the Web' to 'Synthesizing Local Solutions' using multimodal AI.
pivot.after: A visual-first, voice-led knowledge engine that provides actionable summaries in regional dialects.

stages.actions: User opens the search bar to find a solution for a crop pest.
stages.friction: Keyboard defaults to English; user struggles to spell the pest name.

stages.actions: User taps voice icon and describes the problem in Hindi.
stages.friction: Background noise interference makes voice recognition difficult.

stages.actions: Engine displays a mix of video tutorials and local expert contacts.
stages.friction: Loading state for high-quality video content on slow networks.

content.layout: Grid-based visual cards with auto-play previews

annotations.title: Vernacular First UI
annotations.rationale: Uses localized terminology instead of translated jargon to reduce cognitive load.

annotations.title: Actionable Summaries
annotations.rationale: AI-generated summaries in bullet points to prevent information overload.

annotations.title: Low-Bandwidth Mode
annotations.rationale: Optimizes image and video assets for 3G/4G connectivity prevalent in rural areas.
